original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Gallstone disease: diagnosis and management — Recommendations
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults with suspected or diagnosed gallstone disease, including after gallbladder or stone treatment.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Supports symptom context, clinician-reviewed liver tests and imaging, established-diagnosis follow-up, and post-treatment symptom documentation without ordering investigations or recommending surgery.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — diagnostic context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting symptoms, liver-test and ultrasound status, and existing investigation context without interpreting results.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.3 — information after treatment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording clinician-entered discussion and post-treatment symptom follow-up without creating advice.

title: Gallstone disease: diagnosis and management — Recommendations
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
population: Adults with suspected or diagnosed gallstone disease, including after gallbladder or stone treatment.
intended_setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
